Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class EmulatorStateViewModel

Namespace
Pandowdy.UI.ViewModels
Assembly
Pandowdy.UI.dll

View model for displaying real-time Apple IIe emulator state (PC, cycles, BASIC line number).

public sealed class EmulatorStateViewModel : ReactiveObject, IReactiveNotifyPropertyChanged<IReactiveObject>, IHandleObservableErrors, IReactiveObject, INotifyPropertyChanged, INotifyPropertyChanging, IEnableLogger, IActivatableViewModel
Inheritance
object
ReactiveObject
EmulatorStateViewModel
Implements
IReactiveNotifyPropertyChanged<IReactiveObject>
IHandleObservableErrors
IReactiveObject
INotifyPropertyChanged
INotifyPropertyChanging
IEnableLogger
IActivatableViewModel

object.GetType()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Purpose: Provides reactive properties bound to UI elements that display the current execution state of the emulator. Updates are automatically pushed from the emulator core via the IEmulatorState stream.

Reactive Pattern: Implements ReactiveUI's IActivatableViewModel pattern for lifecycle-aware subscriptions. The view model only subscribes to the state stream when activated (view is visible), preventing unnecessary updates and resource usage.

Update Frequency: Samples the emulator state stream at 50ms intervals (20 Hz) to balance UI responsiveness with performance. This prevents excessive UI updates while still providing smooth feedback during emulation.

Thread Safety: All property updates are marshaled to the UI thread via RxApp.MainThreadScheduler, ensuring safe binding to UI elements.

Typical Usage: 

<TextBlock Text="{Binding PC, StringFormat='PC: ${0:X4}'}" />
<TextBlock Text="{Binding Cycles, StringFormat='Cycles: {0:N0}'}" />
<TextBlock Text="{Binding LineNumber, StringFormat='Line: {0}'}" />

Constructors

EmulatorStateViewModel(IEmulatorState)

Initializes a new instance of the EmulatorStateViewModel class.

public EmulatorStateViewModel(IEmulatorState state)

Parameters

state IEmulatorState

Emulator state provider supplying reactive state updates.

Remarks

Initialization: The constructor sets up a reactive subscription using ReactiveUI's WhenActivated pattern. The actual subscription to the state stream is deferred until the view model is activated (typically when the view becomes visible).

Subscription Lifecycle:

  1. Activation: When view becomes visible, WhenActivated callback fires
  2. Subscription: Subscribe to state stream with 50ms sampling
  3. Update Properties: PC, LineNumber, Cycles updated on UI thread
  4. Deactivation: When view is hidden/disposed, subscription is automatically disposed

Sampling Strategy: Uses Observable.Sample(50ms) to throttle updates to 20 Hz. This prevents excessive UI updates (emulator can generate updates at much higher frequency) while maintaining smooth visual feedback.

Thread Marshaling: ObserveOn(RxApp.MainThreadScheduler) ensures all property updates occur on the UI thread, which is required for binding to UI elements.

Memory Management: The WhenActivated pattern automatically handles subscription disposal, preventing memory leaks when the view model is no longer active.

Exceptions

ArgumentNullException

Thrown if state is null.

Properties

Activator

Gets the view model activator for managing subscription lifecycle.

public ViewModelActivator Activator { get; }

Property Value

ViewModelActivator

Remarks

Used by ReactiveUI's WhenActivated mechanism to automatically subscribe when the view model is active (view visible) and unsubscribe when inactive (view hidden/disposed). This prevents memory leaks and unnecessary processing.

Cycles

Gets the total number of CPU cycles executed since the last reset.

public ulong Cycles { get; }

Property Value

ulong

Cycle count (0 to ulong.MaxValue), increments at 1.023 MHz rate.

Remarks

Cycle Timing: The Apple IIe runs at approximately 1,023,000 Hz (1.023 MHz). This property shows the total number of cycles executed, which can be used to:

  • Calculate elapsed time: cycles / 1,023,000 = seconds
  • Measure performance of BASIC or machine code programs
  • Verify emulator timing accuracy
  • Display uptime or execution duration

Example Values:

  • 1,023,000 cycles ≈ 1 second of emulation
  • 17,063 cycles ≈ 1 video frame (~60 Hz)
  • 61,380,000 cycles ≈ 1 minute of emulation

Reset Behavior: Reset to 0 on full system reset (power cycle). Continues counting during warm reset (Ctrl+Reset).

LineNumber

Gets the current Applesoft BASIC line number being executed, or null if not in BASIC.

public int? LineNumber { get; }

Property Value

int?

Line number (0-63999) if executing BASIC code, null if in Monitor or machine code.

Remarks

Detection Method: The emulator reads zero page locations $75-$76 which contain the BASIC line pointer. If the value is less than $FA00, it's interpreted as a valid BASIC line number.

Use Cases:

  • Display current line during BASIC program execution
  • Help users debug BASIC programs by showing execution progress
  • Null when in Monitor, running machine code, or in immediate mode

Binding Example: 

<TextBlock Text="{Binding LineNumber, StringFormat='Line: {0}', 
                         TargetNullValue='(not in BASIC)'}" />

PC

Gets the current 6502 Program Counter (PC) register value.

public ushort PC { get; }

Property Value

ushort

16-bit address ($0000-$FFFF) indicating the next instruction to execute.

Remarks

The PC points to the memory address of the next instruction the CPU will execute. In the Apple IIe, common PC values include:

  • $F666: Monitor prompt
  • $E000-$FFFF: ROM (Monitor, BASIC interpreter)
  • $0800-$BFFF: User program space

Updates approximately 20 times per second (50ms sample interval) when the view model is active.
